title: Fordo
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Alpha}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image=[[File:FordoHelmetshot.jpg]] |name=Fordo
Alpha-77 |homeworld=[[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]{{DB|organization|clonetroopers|clone troopers}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Cloning/Legends|clone]]}}{{InsiderCite|84|Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |donor=[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] |height=1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]]{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Vintage Collection|pack=#054: ARC Trooper Commander ("Captain Fordo")|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TVC54ARCFordo.asp}} |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[The Muunilinst 10]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Three life signs. One in the rubble. Another in the rafters. Everyone else, with me. Move out!|Fordo, during the Battle of Hypori|audio=ThreeLifeSigns.flac|{{CW|21}}}} '''Fordo''', also known by the designations '''Alpha-77''' and '''ARC-77''', was an [[Alpha-class Advanced Recon Commando|Alpha-class]] [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commando]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] born on [[Kamino]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], he was assigned to a task force led by the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], to take the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]]-held [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]]. Together with a group of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] that would later come to be known as [[The Muunilinst 10]], Fordo proved instrumental in the [[Battle of Muunilinst/Legends|Battle of Muunilinst]], leading his [[Troop/Legends|troops]] to destroy an enemy artillery emplacement, and then helping Kenobi capture the enemy leader, [[San Hill/Legends|San Hill]]. Immediately after the battle was over, Fordo was dispatched to the planet [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]], where a group of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knights had been trapped by the Confederate General [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. Making their way to the Jedi's location, Fordo and his men battled Grievous, but were unable to [[Death/Legends|kill]] him. They were, however, able to recover three survivors: [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]], [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]], and [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Fordo was on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] when the planet was [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|attacked]] by the Confederacy. Despite facing overwhelming numbers, Fordo and his men were able to beat back a portion of the attacking force in [[Sector Four (Coruscant)|Sector Four]] with the help of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] and [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. ==Biography== ===Muunilinst=== {{Quote|Now, we wait for General Kenobi.|Fordo, to his team, The Muunilinst 10|[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Cine-Manga|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Cine-Manga]]}} [[File:FordoMunny.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Fordo clears the area around the turret.]] An [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commando]], ARC-77 was a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] for service in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. Becoming a [[Clone Captain/Legends|captain]], he was given the name "Fordo." In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], in the opening stages of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] led an army to the planet [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]], where the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] was manufacturing substantial [[Droid/Legends|droid]] armies for the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].{{CW|1}} Fordo was among the [[Troop/Legends|troops]] sent, and he was to lead a team of two ARC [[Clone Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenants]] and seven infantry [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], making up the group that would come to be known as [[The Muunilinst 10]]. Their objective was to make their way to the headquarters of Banking Clan leader [[San Hill/Legends|San Hill]], independently from the rest of Kenobi's forces; they would rendezvous with Kenobi there. Before entering the [[Star system/Legends|system]], Kenobi personally assured Fordo and his men, reminding them that their mission was of the utmost importance in the greater context of the upcoming [[Battle of Muunilinst/Legends|Battle of Muunilinst]].{{CW|2}} For the engagement, Fordo was given the title of [[Task Force Commander]]. Fordo and his men made their way to the planet surface in a [[Muunilinst 10 LAAT|Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry]], as did most of Kenobi's forces, while [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and his [[Clone trooper pilot/Legends|pilots]] engaged the Banking Clan's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. Before reaching the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Harnaidan]], Kenobi ordered the ARC [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] to break off, and begin their operation. As they made their way into the city separately, their ship was shot down, and crashed in an abandoned street. Fordo and his men were unharmed, however, and he ordered them to debark, with caution. They soon came under [[Sniper/Legends|sniper]] fire, however, and [[CT-43/002]] was lost in the attack. Taking cover behind what remained of the gunship, the [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] had a spotter locate where the snipers were. Once their locations were determined, Fordo had one of his [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenants]] use a [[PLX-1/Legends|PLX-1 portable missile launcher]] to wipe them out utterly, causing considerable damage to the surrounding architecture.{{CW|3}} Once the area was clear, Fordo led his men through the city streets, towards Hill's headquarters. Along the way, they encountered an [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tank]], but one of the lieutenants was able to dispatch it single-handedly. Using a [[commando recon droid]], Fordo determined the target's location, and spotted a [[Harnaidan defensive cannon|large cannon]] at the base of Hill's tower. Reporting to Kenobi, the Captain was given permission to proceed. Fordo led his troops to the base of the tower, and then, along with them, rappelled up to the position of the cannon. The clone troopers quickly dispatched the droid guard stationed there, and one of the lieutenants destroyed the visible command booth at the top of the tower with his PLX-1. The Captain's men then placed a large amount of explosives, rappelling to the top of the tower as the cannon exploded below. At the top of the tower, they waited for the arrival of [[General/Legends|General]] Kenobi. [[File:Muunilinst10.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Fordo and The Muunilinst 10]] As planned, Kenobi rendezvoused with them at the top of Hill's tower. Blowing their way through the roof, Fordo and his men barged into Hill's war room, summarily destroying his droids with the help of the General. When the fighting was over, Fordo grabbed Hill, and brought him before Kenobi. As the General requested an unconditional surrender from Hill, the [[Gen'Dai/Legends|Gen'Dai]] Separatist [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Durge/Legends|Durge]] entered the room on a [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]]. Fordo and his men quickly concentrated their fire on the newcomer, and were able to down him, before bombarding him with even more blaster fire. When one of the lieutenants used his PLX-1 on Durge, Fordo was convinced that the Gen'Dai had been neutralized. Kenobi was not so sure, however, and his caution was proved wise: Durge recovered from the attack, and began dispatching Fordo's troops.{{CW|9}} The hulking Gen'Dai then "swallowed" Kenobi, trapping the General. Fordo, in response, fired his wrist [[Grappling hook/Legends|grappling hook]] at Durge's helmet and electrified it, angering the Separatist commander. Durge was able to break the connection, knocking Fordo back and leaving him vulnerable to attack. Before the Gen'Dai could kill the Captain, however, Kenobi, from within Durge's form, pushed outwards with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], seemingly destroying the attacker and freeing himself. The skirmish over, Fordo and his men rounded up Hill and his aides. When the battle was finally over, the Captain joined Kenobi in overseeing the aftermath, whereupon an [[Ginivex-class starfighter/Legends|unidentified ship]] approached their position. The ship had, during the space battle over Muunilinst, fled to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and had subsequently been chased by Commander Skywalker. As it came down to land, Kenobi, sensing Skywalker aboard, ordered his men to hold their fire. While Kenobi berated Skywalker for following the ship, he received a transmission from General [[Daakman Barrek]], who, along with other [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], required evacuation from [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]], where they had been outflanked by the Separatist General [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]].{{CW|20}} ===Hypori=== {{Quote|Approaching droid perimeter. We are undetected.''"
"''Gun it!''"
"''Sir!|A clone pilot and Fordo|}} [[File:FordoAndBuddies.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Fordo and his men engage Grievous on Hypori.]] Fordo was sent to Hypori to rescue the survivors of Barrek's task force. He and ten lieutenants were tasked with recovering them, and, favoring a more aggressive approach, Fordo brought along an [[Advanced Recon Commando heavy gunner]], and a [[Reciprocating quad blaster cannon/Legends|reciprocating quad blaster]]. Reaching Hypori in a gunship, Fordo and his men approached the downed [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]] in which the surviving Jedi were hiding, and the Captain, after preparing his gear, ordered his pilot to begin jamming the enemy. Making its way past the army of droids that surrounded the assault ship, the gunship dropped remote bombs amongst the enemy, before blasting its way into the Acclamator. As soon as he debarked, Fordo detected three life signs and had one of his troops check in some nearby rubble, while another checked in the rafters. Making his way towards the life signs with all due haste, Fordo progressively ordered his troops to fan out. They were met with the sight of a wounded [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], General [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]], dueling General Grievous. Fordo and his men wasted no time in opening fire on the cyborg General, but Grievous was able to evade their shots and began cutting down the ARC troopers. The Captain reacted by having his [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|gunship]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] blast his way into the area, and open fire on Grievous with the ship's weapons. While the cyborg fled the site, Fordo's men recovered the other surviving Jedi: [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]] and [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]]. When the Captain ordered his men back on board the gunship, Ki-Adi-Mundi protested, insisting that they pursue Grievous, but Fordo pointed out that the survivors would surely perish if they lingered on Hypori. The General relented, and boarded the craft. As they flew away from the crashed Acclamator, Fordo activated the remote bombs, wiping out a large number of enemy troops and clearing a path for the gunship to escape through. The wounded Jedi, though in a critical condition, survived, and were able to return to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] capital of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Due to his efforts, Fordo was awarded the [[Chancellor's Service Medal]], but he refused, and had the honor bestowed upon the late [[CT-43/002]], one of his men who had [[Death/Legends|perished]] during the Battle of Muunilinst. ===Coruscant=== {{Quote|Sector Four needs reinforcements. We are being overrun. Repeat, we are—|Fordo|{{CW|23}}}} [[File:Fordoblaster.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fordo during the Battle of Coruscant.|link=Special:FilePath/Fordoblaster.jpg]] In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Fordo was on Coruscant when it was [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|attacked]] by the Confederacy. Defending the [[Sector Four (Coruscant)|Sector Four]] district, Fordo was hard pressed to beat back the attackers, and he realized that his position was being overrun. Calling for reinforcements, he took his [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15A blaster rifle]] and used it to smack down an approaching [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droid]]. Drawing his pistol and picking up a fallen comrade's DC-15, Fordo continued to hold his ground, but eventually gave the order to retreat. At that moment, Jedi Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] arrived astride a [[Kybuck/Legends|kybuck]], and countermanded the Captain's earlier order. Shortly afterward, General [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] arrived astride a commandeered [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|vulture droid]]. The battle continued on, and Fordo's men gained the advantage, allowing the Captain to have his right flank advance into the enemy lines. He did not have to worry about his left flank, however, as that was being watched over by Windu and Yoda.{{CW|25}} The Republic ended up winning the Battle of Coruscant.{{Film|III}} ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Forward! Keep up the pressure on the right!''"
"''Sir, what about our left flank?''"
"''The Jedi have the left.|Fordo and a clone trooper, during the Battle of Coruscant|}} Far from being a subtle man, Fordo preferred straightforward tactics, employing overwhelming firepower as opposed to complicated strategies. He eventually became famous for his bombastic approach to any given situation, which continually awarded him success. Preferring actions over words, Fordo rarely spoke unless he was being addressed to by superior officers and would generally give orders via hand signals and gestures. He was also modest, refusing to take credit for his actions, and instead valuing the contributions of those who fell on the field of battle more. Fordo was undeterred in the face of great danger, and fought on with unfailing courage{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Clone Wars (toy line)|packtype=Battle Pack|pack=ARC Troopers|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TCWBPARCTroopers.asp}} no matter how dire the situation, although he would give the order to retreat in the face of overwhelming odds. He would also disobey his superiors if they were to jeopardize his objectives, as he did with General Ki-Adi-Mundi on Hypori. However, during the Battle of Coruscant, Fordo had faith within Jedi Generals Mace Windu and Yoda, who both oversaw the Republic troops' left flank. ==Equipment== [[File:FordoBlaster-CW21.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Fordo charging his DC-15S]] Early in the Clone Wars, Fordo wore a lighter version of the [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase I clone armor]] called [[ARC trooper armor/Legends|ARC trooper armor]], which also gave Fordo more protection than its Phase I counterpart.''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' Fordo's armor itself sported red markings. The set also featured an array of equipment, such as a grappling hook and [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] system built into his left gauntlet, a blast-dampening gray [[Pauldron/Legends|pauldron]], a skirt-like [[Kama/Legends|''kama'']], and a [[Utility belt/Legends|belt]] that featured a dual pouch [[Holster/Legends|holster]] that held his custom twin [[DC-15S/Legends|DC-15s blasters]]. His pauldron had an ammunition pouch attachment, which held ammo for his twin [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]]. While clones under his command utilized other weapons at their disposal, Fordo preferred to use his twin hand [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], which he used during the Battles of Muunilinst and Hypori. During the Battle of Coruscant, Fordo used only one hand blaster and a [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15A blaster rifle]]. Fordo's helmet featured a [[Rangefinder/Legends|rangefinder]]. He also had a portable [[holograph]]ic projector, which he utilized during the Battle of Muunilinst, and a pair of white [[Macrobinoculars/Legends|macrobinoculars]]. During the later days of the Clone Wars, Fordo, like many clone troopers, switched to the [[Phase II clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase II]] version of the clone armor. Fordo's Phase II armor sported red markings and was similar to his Phase I ARC trooper armor, although the set did not feature his ''kama'', his pauldron ammunition pouch attachment, nor his dual pouch blaster holsters. Instead, Fordo sported a bandolier and a belt that had one pouch holster for a blaster pistol. Fordo also transitioned to using a BARC trooper helmet which did not feature a rangefinder, but instead featured [[Jaig eyes|''Jaig'' eyes]], an honor he earned as a result of his heroic actions on Hypori.{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/84/indexp2.html|text=''Behind the Armor: A Clones Primer''|int=Behind the Armor: A Clones Primer}} Like his Phase I armor, however, Fordo's left arm gauntlet had a built-in comlink system. ==Behind the scenes== ===Portrayal=== {{Quote|The ''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' animated shorts depicted soldiers in the franchise in a way they'd never been seen before. The clone troopers depicted therein weren't faceless cannon fodder. Oh, the "faceless" part was still true, sure—but they had personality, and they moved with heroic freneticism, charging and blasting and moving with their squadmates in the larger-than-life fashion we'd only ever seen from Jedi and Sith. ''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' made the clones cool. ARC-77, a.k.a. "Fordo" a.k.a. "That awesome clone in the armor with red highlights" led his Advanced Recon Commando troops to victory alongside Jedi and stood face-to-face with General Grievous. The truth is, I don't remember him for his professional affect or occasional sly flourish—I remember him for introducing a fresh approach to Star Wars combat.|Alexander Freed|{{InsiderCite|163|Red Five|Red Five: Alexander Freed}}}} The character of Fordo made his first appearance in the [[Chapter 2 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|second episode]] of the series ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]''. He later appeared in the [[Clone Wars: Season Two|second season]] of the show, and was voiced by [[André Sogliuzzo]]. He was eventually named in the article "''[[Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic]]''," which was published in [[Star Wars Insider 84|''Star Wars Insider'' 84]]. Author [[Alexander Freed]] spoke highly of the character for introducing a new approach to ''[[Star Wars]]'' combat, listing him as one of his top five ''Star Wars'' soldiers in [[Star Wars Insider 163|''Star Wars Insider'' 163]]. Within, he argued that Fordo and his fellow ''Clone Wars'' clones had helped bring personality to the otherwise faceless ranks and praised the show's "larger-than-life" action. All the same, while he wrote that Fordo had "style," he also wrote that Clone Captain [[Rex/Legends|Rex]] provided more character "substance" than that exhibited by Fordo. ===Inconsistencies=== {{Quote|Commander, we're approaching target.''"
"''Begin jamming signals.''"
"''Yes, sir!|A clone pilot calls Fordo "commander" in the twenty first chapter of the ''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' series|}} There have been a few inconsistencies with his rank. At times Fordo was referred to as "commander." In particular, Obi-Wan Kenobi called Fordo a commander in the [[Chapter 9 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|ninth chapter]] of the ''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' series. In the [[Chapter 21 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|twenty first chapter]] of the series, a clone trooper called Fordo a commander as well. In the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Cine-Manga|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' PhotoComic]], a text box above Fordo reads as "The ARC trooper commander gives the all-clear signal." A few pages after the discrepancy, Kenobi referred to Fordo as a "sergeant." The issue with Fordo being called a commander was later clarified in the ''[[Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic]]'', a short story featured in [[Star Wars Insider 84|''Star Wars Insider'' 84]], which was penned by authors [[Karen Traviss]] and [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]]. The guide mentioned that Fordo received the designation "Task Force Commander" during the Battle of Muunilinst. The designation itself was a title, not military rank. The guide also introduced Fordo's captain rank. His captain rank, as well as his Task Force Commander title, was mentioned in his entry in the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.{{CSWECite|I|288|Fordo}} The rank appeared in two [[Hasbro]] toy figure packs featuring Fordo, released in [[2010]] and [[2011]], respectively. Because later sources claim that Fordo was indeed a captain, this article assumes Fordo's rank was captain. For the ongoing [[Star Wars: The Vintage Collection|''Vintage Collection'' toy line]] by Hasbro, Fordo was released with both his Phase I and II equipment as "ARC Trooper Commander" in 2011. His figure features a unique facesculpt with a small chin beard. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Fordo}} *[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Cine-Manga|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Cine-Manga]] *{{CW|2}} {{1st}} *{{CW|3}} *{{CW|8}} *{{CW|9}} *{{CW|20}} *{{CW|21}} *{{CW|23}} *{{CW|25}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|84|Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic}} {{1stID|ARC-77, Fordo}} *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/84/indexp2.html|text=''Behind the Armor: A Clones Primer''|int=Behind the Armor: A Clones Primer}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{Imo}} *{{SWArchive|url=welcome/about/news/f20051223/index.html|text=''Star Wars:'' The Best of 2005}} *{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Saga Collection|packtype=Battle Pack|pack=The Hunt for Grievous|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tsc06BPhuntforgrievous.asp}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{SWArchive|url=vault/collecting/20090109.html|text=Red, White and Cool}} *''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]'' {{Imo}} *{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Clone Wars (toy line)|packtype=Battle Pack|pack=ARC Troopers|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TCWBPARCTroopers.asp}} *{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Vintage Collection|pack=#054: ARC Trooper Commander ("Captain Fordo")|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TVC54ARCFordo.asp}} *{{InsiderCite|163|Red Five|Red Five: Alexander Freed}} *{{InsiderCite|215|The Galactic Gallery: Inside the Star Wars Celebration 2022 Art Show|The Galactic Gallery: Inside the ''Star Wars'' Celebration 2022 Art Show}} *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-micro-galaxy-squadron-series-3|text=Punch It! First Look at Jazwares' ''Star Wars'' Micro Galaxy Squadron Series III}} *{{GentleGiantCite|set=Premier Collection Statues|pack=Captain Fordo|link=star-wars-the-clone-wars-captain-fordo-premier-collection-statue}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Alpha-77 |es=ARC-77 |fi=ARC-77 |fr=Fordo |nl=Alpha-77 |pl=Alpha-77 |pt=Legends:ARC-77 |tr=Fordo |uk=Фордо |ptGA=1 }} [[Category:Alpha-class Advanced Recon Commandos]] [[Category:Clone trooper captains]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Members of the Muunilinst 10]]
